subject: A flowing cloak or mantle made of living water, billowing in midair as if worn by an invisible figure. The garment is woven from currents, foam, and translucent ribbons of sea, with no wearer visible.
description: A dramatic mantle of pure water hangs suspended against a deeper aquatic backdrop. Translucent folds, ribbons, and currents form the shape of a long flowing cape or robe, with whitecap foam edges and crashing wave details suggesting a collar and sleeves. The garment is unworn — it floats on its own as a magical artifact, animated by oceanic force. The background is layered cyan and deep blue water, with diffuse light filtering through from above. The composition centers the empty cloak head-on, with no human or creature figure present.
foreground: a flowing cloak/mantle composed entirely of water and wave currents, suspended in midair
background: deeper turquoise and indigo waters with shafts of filtered light
emotion: stillness with implied motion; eerie absence of a wearer
